item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be found as cc-compliant under any source found, and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great example of a well-done "series 1 idea." A simple idea, competent and concise execution, a strong sense of internal logic, a striking image, and a sense of danger and mystery. +1

item.title: Fermi Paradox
item.content:encoded:
Long term lurker here. Not much to add but this got my Fermi Paradox sense twinkling.
In rl the Breakthrough Starshot https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Breakthrough_Starshot Project hopes to use technology expected within the next 50 years to build an interstellar laser sail with a tiny probe.
If other civilisations can manage similar who is to say they couldn't fire off a self replicating "brick" capable of building a structure on a target planet big enough to send a simple message for telescopes home (such as an 12 pointed star for "danger here" or "anomalous planet")
If anybody wants to take this idea further feel free, I find the EDIT button here a terrifying thing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i liked it, theres precious few things that don't just come across evidence of human life and proceed to make it wish it could kill itself on this site, i really like the idea that just a two piece shack can stop it, awesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As least it isn't found "by a Foundation agent working undercover in the Andes mountain patrol service".
Anyway, it's well written, so naturally…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, to the point, fairly original, good origin story, interesting effect, reasonable.
Checks all the boxes. Has everything a good skip needs. Over a hundred is very good, but really, the only reason it's not higher rated is that it doesn't stir up the controversy. No format screws, no epic tale, not a cause of a massive death toll. Just some weird thing you put in a box and forget.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably one of the more better simple items on the site. Really cool effects that are dangerous without crossing the line into "Mega Ultra Danger" territory, its logic is consistent instead of being random and all over the place, and nothing more is there than needed to be. For something that was around since the site first came into being, and even by now, this is really damn good. My only issue is the dubious discovery story, as the "found by luck/chance" thing always rubbed me the wrong way. Otherwise, I enjoy this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, it leaves me wanting more… in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As others have said, I am particular to this one and like the idea the author came up with, especially considering if the inspiration was the picture of a brick. Reading this makes me want to allow it to grow as large as possible and move anything out of its way to ensure such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda like this one. It's got a sense of mystery to it, as well as a sense of "what might happen if…?"

Personally, I'd like to see what happens if someone chucks it on Mars or the Moon or something. Give it an entire world to grow on with nothing manmade to get in its way. Could be quite interesting…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one even though it is not creepy. It has a sense of mystery to it, and makes me think of the Nazca Plains pictograms.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: And I want to introduce an SCP-611-imprinted child to SCP-263, but they won't let me do that any time soon, either, will they? :P

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Personally, I'd like to just set this fucker down right in the middle of the Bonneville Salt Flats and see what comes up. Not a chance in hell, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this thing forms a compass rose for map photographers?

item.title: So if I have the timeline right...
item.content:encoded:
1. SCP notices a replicating structure.
  2. SCP removes the "cornerstone" (inadvertantly halting growth).
  3. SCP figures out what's going on.
  4. SCP decides to let the brick finish somewhere else.

Am I correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah. Sorry about the numbers thing. A habit of mine.

item.title: Very cool
item.content:encoded: I like this one a lot. Not dangerous, but still very interesting. I wonder if, after it finished one structure, it would start another, eventually covering everything in these building structures. That is, assuming nothing got in its way…
